prompt:
I look after the equipment depot systems for a ranger district and I've inherited three copy-pasted "repositories" — one for radios, one for trail cameras, one for bear canisters — that are the same 80 lines with the types swapped and slightly different bugs. I'm consolidating on ONE generic contract before we add the fourth equipment type. C# / .NET 10, namespace GearRepo, single project. Here's the design I want, and I do mean the constraint clauses and the default interface members — the convenience layer belongs ON the interface so every future backend (sqlite, the sync service) inherits it for free instead of re-implementing it. The test suite reflects over the interface to make sure of exactly that, so don't quietly move those bodies into the class.

interface IEntity<TKey> where TKey : notnull, IEquatable<TKey> — just TKey Id { get; }. Our entities are records; a positional Id component must satisfy it.

sealed class DuplicateKeyException : InvalidOperationException — thrown on any key collision, Message exactly "id 'R-14' already present" (same shape for any id, including int keys).

interface IRepository<T, TKey> where T : class, IEntity<TKey> where TKey : notnull, IEquatable<TKey>. Abstract storage members (a backend MUST implement these): void Add(T item) — throws DuplicateKeyException on an existing key; bool TryGet(TKey id, out T item) — use [MaybeNullWhen(false)] on the out so callers get clean nullability; bool Remove(TKey id) — true only if something was removed; IReadOnlyList<T> Snapshot() — a COPY in insertion order (re-adding a removed id puts it at the end; later mutation must not bleed into an already-taken snapshot); int Count { get; }. Default interface members (bodies on the interface, built only from the abstract members): bool Exists(TKey id); T GetRequired(TKey id) — returns or throws KeyNotFoundException with Message exactly "no entity with id 'R-99'" (shape for any id); void AddRange(IEnumerable<T> items) — appends in sequence order, but ATOMICALLY: check the whole batch first (against the store AND for duplicates inside the batch itself) and if anything collides throw DuplicateKeyException and add NOTHING; IReadOnlyList<T> FindAll(Func<T, bool> predicate) — matches in insertion order.

class InMemoryRepository<T, TKey> : IRepository<T, TKey> — dictionary-backed, implements just the abstract storage members.

static class DepotAudit with static IReadOnlyList<TKey> MissingIds<T, TKey>(IRepository<T, TKey> repo, IEnumerable<TKey> expected) (same constraints) — the intake clipboard check: which expected ids are NOT in the repo, first-occurrence order, no duplicates in the result.

GearRepoTests.cs is the acceptance contract — it runs the whole thing against a string-keyed record AND an int-keyed record. Don't touch the tests, the csproj, or .gitignore. `dotnet test` green means done.
